open_question_resolutions: Architect recommendations R-1 through R-6 accepted as-is (routine per escalation policy): minimal --explain rendering; now-parameter clock injection; recency rules default warn with documented done-blocking opt-in; defer cross-rule index caching; pin LLM eval model in README; reserve scope=all for label-uniqueness only.
escalations: bc86f54c neighborhood criterion literally unsatisfiable without reverse-edge index; user approved amending the criterion (2026-06-10).
bc86f54c same-finding-class x3 (transition paths bypassing enforcement); user chose chokepoint refactor (apply_state_transition), which closed the class.
traps: Workers must NEVER git-restore/stage anything under .jit/ - rework2-cca3e80b's restore wiped the lead's recorded gate results (re-run was cheap this time; may not be next time). Dispatch prompts now must say: never run git restore on .jit paths.

code-review gate enforces literal acceptance-criteria readings: --explain criterion required visible non-match reporting (R-1 minimal option was insufficient); 'instance path on findings' applies to specialized messages too; public APIs need # Examples doctests.
version_cli_tests flake under full parallel cargo test (binary-build contention + aws-lc-sys in no-network sandbox); pass serially - do not chase as regressions.

All issue state changes MUST route through CommandExecutor::apply_state_transition (commands/mod.rs). Direct issue.state writes in command code are the recurring review-blocker class; the only sanctioned exceptions are documented inline (create initial state, dependency.rs ready->backlog demotion, validation projections).

The code-review gate keeps finding the next unguarded transition path; before re-running it after transition-semantics changes, grep commands/ for '.state = ' and check each site routes through the chokepoint.
